title: How French courts became Europe's toughest on women returning from IS
subhead: Of the 23 terrorism trials scheduled in French courts in 2026, 18 involve women who joined the Islamic State organization in Syria. While their role was once overlooked, they are now being judged on equal footing with men.

title: Marjane Satrapi, author of 'Persepolis,' dies at 56
'The current war between Iran and the US stems from decades of mutual humiliation'
Eating organic fruits and vegetables linked to lower risk of postmenopausal breast cancer
subhead: According to French research, for every daily 100-gram portion of conventionally grown fruits and vegetables replaced by their organic equivalents, the risk of developing postmenopausal breast cancer drops by 10%.

title: Trump confirms mass rally and scraps planned concerts for America's 250th anniversary
subhead: Multiple musical acts pulled out of planned performances for the mass rally in Washington on June 25, citing the event's politicization. Trump announced on Thursday that he had told these 'singers with no talent (...) to stay home.'

title: Artist Louise Vo Tan brings the Crown Jewels, stolen from the Louvre, back to life
subhead: 'They were dazzling, like meringue,' said Vo Tan, who filmed the museum's treasures before they were stolen, and is now displaying them in hologram form in the building once used for the Port-Coton foghorn.

title: Thomas Piketty's lab proposes ambitious plan to cut global inequality and curb climate change
subhead: Building a more equitable world while respecting planetary limits is possible, economists from the World Inequality Lab assert in a report published Thursday. To achieve this, they say, targeted reductions in consumption in specific sectors, a drastic cut in fossil fuel use and changes in dietary habits will be necessary.

'Most PFAS emissions in Europe come from air conditioners and heat pumps'
subhead: The debate over these chemical substances is being reignited within the European Union, as machines designed to help adapt to climate change come under fire, historian Jean-Baptiste Fressoz notes in his column.

title: The health of French presidents, a state secret
subhead: A new book unveils the last mysteries surrounding the illnesses of French presidents, especially that of Georges Pompidou (1969-1974), drawing on the diary of one of the president's physicians, Jean Bernard.

title: Marjane Satrapi: 'At age 10, I was training myself to become a political prisoner'
subhead: The Franco-Iranian artist, who gained worldwide recognition with the graphic novel 'Persepolis,' died at 56 years old, her family announced Thursday. Le Monde interviewed her in October 2020. We are republishing that interview today.
